question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that viral RNA was determined in 160 baseline plasma samples from HIV-1 chronically infected treatment naïve patients. This indicates that HIV sequences were indeed obtained from patient samples.
sentences: Viral RNA was determined in 160 baseline plasma samples; One hundred and sixty samples (73 males and 87 females) were amplified and sequenced for HIV-1 subtyping and genotypic drug resistance analysis.

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper focuses on genotypic drug resistance analysis and does not mention any in vitro drug susceptibility testing. The methods and results sections describe sequencing and mutation analysis but do not include any in vitro assays to test drug susceptibility.
sentences: The entire PR and the first 335 codons (76%) of the RT regions of the pol gene of the HIV-1 genome of 160 patients were amplified and sequenced using an in-house assay; Genotypic drug resistance was defined as the presence of one or more resistance-related mutations as specified by the consensus mutation of Stanford University HIVDB and the International Antiviral Society (IAS) mutation lists.

question: What were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The paper explicitly states that the nucleotide sequences are deposited in the National Centre for Biotechnology Information (NCBI), USA GenBank with the accession numbers KF026059-KF026220.
sentences: Nucleotide sequences are deposited in National Centre for Biotechnology Information (NCBI), USA GenBank (Accession Number: KF026059-KF026220).

question: Which HIV species were studied in the paper?
explain: The paper consistently refers to HIV-1 throughout the text, including in the title, abstract, and methods sections. There is no mention of HIV-2 or any other species.
sentences: This study was aimed to determine the molecular characterization and pre-antiretroviral treatment resistance mutations among HIV-1 chronically infected ART naïve patients after the roll out of ART in Ethiopia; The entire PR and the first 335 codons (76%) of the RT regions of the pol gene of the HIV-1 genome of 160 patients were amplified and sequenced using an in-house assay.

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
explain: The paper specifies that the entire PR and the first 335 codons (76%) of the RT regions of the pol gene of the HIV-1 genome were amplified and sequenced.
sentences: The entire PR and the first 335 codons (76%) of the RT regions of the pol gene of the HIV-1 genome of 160 patients were amplified and sequenced using an in-house assay.

question: From which countries were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The paper mentions that the study was conducted on patients seeking care and treatment at Gondar University Hospital, Northwest Ethiopia. This indicates that the sequenced samples were obtained from Ethiopia.
sentences: HIV-1 chronically infected treatment naïve patients (N = 160) with advanced diseases (WHO clinical stages III and IV) above 18 years of age and seeking care and treatment at Gondar University Hospital for the first time, Northwest Ethiopia in 2008/2009 were recruited consecutively.

question: From what years were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The paper states that the patients were recruited consecutively in 2008/2009, indicating that the sequenced samples were obtained during these years.
sentences: HIV-1 chronically infected treatment naïve patients (N = 160) with advanced diseases (WHO clinical stages III and IV) above 18 years of age and seeking care and treatment at Gondar University Hospital for the first time, Northwest Ethiopia in 2008/2009 were recruited consecutively.

question: What method was used for sequencing?
explain: The paper describes the use of the Big Dye Terminator Cycle Sequencing Ready Reaction kit (Applied Biosystems), which is a method associated with Sanger sequencing.
sentences: Purified PCR products were subjected to direct sequencing of both sense and antisense strands using Big Dye Terminator Cycle Sequencing Ready Reaction kit (Applied Biosystems, Foster City, CA, USA).

question: Were samples cloned prior to sequencing?
explain: The paper does not mention any cloning of samples prior to sequencing. It describes direct sequencing of PCR products, which implies that cloning was not performed.
